title: Palicourea dolichantha
intro: Palicourea dolichantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as a shrub or tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea dolichantha include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and cytotoxic activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as to support immune function.
